title: Due Scheduling, Monotonic Clocks, Backoff, Jitter, and Pause Policy · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed operations review reconstructs due scheduling, monotonic clocks, backoff, jitter, and pause policy from revision, tenant, source URL, request validators, response bytes, parsed identity, transaction, lease, state transition, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for deterministic adaptive polling scheduler after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Persist next attempt, last success, failure streak, publisher hints, pause state, and scheduling reason independently from process memory.
Inject wall and monotonic time roles, define inclusive due comparisons, and test forward, backward, and boundary changes.
Apply bounded exponential backoff with deterministic-testable jitter, reset rules, Retry-After, and maximum staleness escalation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A review Go team handles case 2447 during a multilingual university archive import. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove persisted due state, injected clocks, adaptive backoff, jitter, pause, and non-overlap; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Persist next attempt, last success, failure streak, publisher hints, pause state, and scheduling reason independently from process memory.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Pause Resume
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Feed Go Pause Resume: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go team handles case 2638 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove persisted due state, injected clocks, adaptive backoff, jitter, pause, and non-overlap; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, date, identity, content, or encoding case and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Make manual, policy, repeated-failure, deletion, and incident pauses explicit, authorized, observable, and safely resumable.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Pause Resume.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Pause Resume, keep Feed Go Pause Resume connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Make manual, policy, repeated-failure, deletion, and incident pauses explicit, authorized, observable, and safely resumable.
